body_excerpt: {"credits": [{"type": "reporter", "user": {"avatar_url": "https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/70614065?v=4", "events_url": "https://api.github.com/users/Doodi101/events{/privacy}", "followers_url": "https://api.github.com/users/Doodi101/followers", "following_url": "https://api.github.com/users/Doodi101/following{/other_user}", "gists_url": "https://api.github.com/users/Doodi101/gists{/gist_id}", "gravatar_id": "", "html_url": "https://github.com/Doodi101", "id": 70614065, "login": "Doodi101", "node_id": "MDQ6VXNlcjcwNjE0MDY1", "organizations_url": "https://api.github.com/users/Doodi101/orgs", "received_events_url": "https://api.github.com/users/Doodi101/received_events", "repos_url": "https://api.github.com/users/Doodi101/repos", "site_admin": false, "starred_url": "https://api.github.com/users/Doodi101/starred{/owner}{/repo}", "subscriptions_url": "https://api.github.com/users/Doodi101/subscriptions", "type": "User", "url": "https://api.github.com/users/Doodi101", "user_view_type": "public"}}], "cve_id": "CVE-2026-46361", "cvss": {"score": 6.9, "vector_string": "CVSS:3.1/AV:N/AC:L/PR:H/UI:R/S:C/C:H/I:L/A:N"}, "cvss_severities": {"cvss_v3": {"score": 6.9, "vector_string": "CVSS:3.1/AV:N/AC:L/PR:H/UI:R/S:C/C:H/I:L/A:N"}, "cvss_v4": {"score": 0.0, "vector_string": null}}, "cwes": [{"cwe_id": "CWE-79", "name": "Improper Neutralization of Input During Web Page Generation ('Cross-site Scripting')"}], "description": "## Summary The search result rendering template (`search.twig`) outputs FAQ content fields `result.question` and `result.answerPreview` using Twig's `| raw` filter, which completely disables the template engine's built-in auto-escaping. A user with FAQ editor/contributor privileges can store a payload encoded as HTML entities. During search result construction, `html_entity_decode(strip_tags(...))` restores the raw HTML tags ŌĆö bypassing `strip_tags()` ŌĆö and the restored payload is injected into every visitor's browser via the `| raw` output. This vulnerability is distinct from GHSA-cv2g-8cj8-vgc7 (affects `faq.twig`, bypass via regex mismatch in `Filter::removeAttributes()`) and is not addressed by the 4.1.1 patch. --- ## Affected Files | File | Location | Issue | |---|---|---| | `phpmyfaq/assets/templates/default/search.twig` | lines rendering `result.question`, `result.answerPreview` | `(Vertical Bar) raw` disables autoescape | | `phpmyfaq/src/phpMyFAQ/Controller/Api/SearchController.php` | search result processing loop | `html_entity_decode(strip_tags(...))` restores encoded payloads | | `phpmyfaq/src/phpMyFAQ/Search.php` | `logSearchTerm()` | No HTML sanitization on stored search term (secondary, preventive) | --- ## Details ### Vulnerability A (Primary): `search.twig` ŌĆö `| raw` Disables Autoescape **File:** `phpmyfaq/assets/templates/default/search.twig` ```twig {{ result.question | raw }} {{ result.answerPreview | raw }}... ``` Twig's autoescape encodes all variables by default. The `| raw` filter unconditionally disables this protection. Both `result.question` and `result.answerPreview` are populated from database content (FAQ records and custom pages) that can contain attacker-controlled data. Seven (7) instances of `| raw` exist in `search.twig`: ```twig {{ result.renderedScore | raw }} {{ result.question | raw }} {{ result.answerPreview | raw }} {{ searchTags | raw }} {{ relatedTags | raw }} {{ pagination | raw }} {{ 'help_search' | translate | raw }} ``` Each of these constitutes an independent XSS surface if its data source is compromised. --- ### Vulnerability B (Amplifier): `SearchController.php` ŌĆö `html_entity_decode(strip_tags())` Bypass **File:** `phpmyfaq/src/phpMyFAQ/Controller/Api/SearchController.php` ```php $data->answer = html_entity_decode( strip_tags((string) $data->answer), ENT_COMPAT, encoding: 'utf-8' ); ``` This pattern is a known security anti-pattern. When a payload is stored as HTML entities, `strip_tags()` passes it through unmodified (it sees no actual tags), and `html_entity_decode()` then restores the original HTML tags ŌĆö reintroducing executable markup that was thought to be neutralized. **Bypass walkthrough:** ```text Stored in DB: ``` **Step 2 ŌĆö Persistence** The payload is stored in the DB without HTML sanitization at the storage layer. **Step 3 ŌĆö Victim triggers the XSS** Any user (including unauthenticated visitors and administrators) searches for a keyword matching the poisoned FAQ. The server: 1. Retrieves the record from the database 2. Applies `strip_tags()` ŌåÆ entity-encoded payload passes through 3. Applies `html_entity_decode()` ŌåÆ raw `{{ result.answerPreview | raw }}... + {{ result.question }} + {{ result.answerPreview }}... ``` If HTML formatting must be preserved, apply a whitelist-based sanitizer (e.g., `ezyang/htmlpurifier`) **before** passing data to the template, then retain `| raw` only for purified output. ### Fix 2 (Critical) ŌĆö Remove `html_entity_decode()` from search result pipeline `SearchController.php` ```diff - $data->answer = html_entity_decode( - strip_tags((string) $data->answer), - ENT_COMPAT, - encoding: 'utf-8' - ); + $data->answer = strip_tags((string) $data->answer); $data->answer = Utils::makeShorterText(string: $data->answer, characters: 12); ``` ### Fix 3 (Recommended) ŌĆö Audit all `| raw` usages in `search.twig` The following additional `| raw` instances should be reviewed and sanitized: ```twig {{ searchTags | raw }} ŌåÆ apply HTML Purifier or remove | raw {{ relatedTags | raw }} ŌåÆ apply HTML Purifier or remove | raw {{ pagination | raw }} ŌåÆ safe only if generated entirely server-side with no user input ``` ### Fix 4 (Preventive) ŌĆö Add `htmlspecialchars()` in `logSearchTerm()` ```diff $this->configuration->getDb()->escape($searchTerm) + htmlspecialchars( + $this->configuration->getDb()->escape($searchTerm), + ENT_QUOTES | ENT_HTML5, + 'UTF-8' + ) ``` ---", "epss": {"percentage": title: phpMyFAQ has stored XSS via | raw Filter in search.twig ŌĆö html_entity_decode(strip_tags()) Bypass in Search Result Rendering
